intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as established specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of extreme senescence or total loss.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss/Critical: The Pothos specimen is not identifiable as a living plant; only substrate and debris remain.

plant_audit.P3: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos has suffered total structural collapse and likely necrosis.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Substrate appears dark and potentially waterlogged or devoid of organic life.
biome_observations.surface_debris: White, crystalline or calcified debris (possibly eggshell fragments) is present in the P3 pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface: No significant biological activity or secondary growth detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: Minimal change; specimens were already in a state of severe decline.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Significant loss of light/contrast; potential camera exposure shift or total plant collapse.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Static state; no recovery or new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered plants have reached a state of total systemic failure. There is no evidence of chlorophyll-based life in any of the pots.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white, jagged debris in P3 is the only non-soil element. No living plant matter is present to reconcile with the registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Across the four registered pots, there is zero evidence of living plant tissue. The Pothos (P3) is marked by the presence of the white rabbit anchor, but the plant itself has vanished or decomposed entirely. The environment is currently a collection of empty, soil-filled vessels.
